skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires AI assistants to verify work with fresh evidence before claiming completion, fixes, passing tests, commits, or pull requests.
skill.summary: Enforces evidence-based completion claims for AI-assisted software work.
skill.author: DYAI2025

security_audit.summary: AI review found no executable code, malicious intent, or prompt-injection attempt in SKILL.md. The static external-command detections are Markdown code fences and examples, while the weak-crypto and reconnaissance flags are ordinary prose. All static findings are assessed as false positives, so the skill is safe to publish.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Backtick Execution
low_findings.description: The flagged locations are Markdown fenced blocks and examples that describe verification behavior. They do not contain Ruby backtick execution, shell invocation, command substitution, or user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines are Markdown fences or example blocks, not executable Ruby. Semantic review found no command string, interpreter call, or automation script.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Description Text
low_findings.description: The line is frontmatter text describing the skill purpose. It contains no hash function, encryption primitive, credential handling, or cryptographic implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is plain metadata text. There is no cryptographic API, algorithm name, or data transformation present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms Used as Process Guidance
low_findings.description: The flagged lines discuss verifying agent reports, trust failures, and implied completion claims. They do not request host enumeration, network discovery, scanning, or collection of system details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are ordinary English guidance inside a verification checklist. No network command, discovery tool, or data exfiltration path appears nearby.

content.value_statement: Teams lose trust when AI assistants claim success without proof. This skill requires fresh verification evidence before completion, commit, or pull request claims.
actual_capabilities: Requires identifying the command or check that proves a status claim.
Directs the assistant to run fresh verification before saying work is complete.
Requires reading command output, exit codes, and failure counts before reporting success.
Defines stricter checks for tests, builds, regression tests, and requirements completion.
Requires independent review of delegated agent reports before trusting their status.
limitations: Does not choose project-specific test, lint, or build commands by itself.
Does not guarantee that existing tests cover every requirement or regression.
Does not automate command execution outside the host AI tool capabilities.
May add extra verification time before status updates or pull requests.

use_cases.title: Pre-PR Verification
use_cases.description: Run the required checks and report evidence before opening or updating a pull request.

use_cases.title: Agent Output Review
use_cases.description: Require AI agents to verify diffs and command results before accepting delegated work as complete.

use_cases.title: Regression Fix Confirmation
use_cases.description: Confirm that a bug fix includes a meaningful red-green regression check before reporting completion.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Completion Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before saying a small task is finished.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before you claim this task is complete, identify the command or check that proves it. Run it, read the full output, and report the evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Test and Build Verification
prompt_templates.prompt: Verify this change before commit. Run the relevant test and build commands, include exit status and failure counts, then state what is proven and what remains unverified.

prompt_templates.title: Regression Evidence Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after fixing a reported bug.
prompt_templates.prompt: Confirm the regression fix with evidence. Show the failing condition or red-green cycle, restore the fix, rerun the check, and report the final result without assumptions.

prompt_templates.title: Delegated Agent Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after another agent reports success.
prompt_templates.prompt: Do not trust the delegated success report by itself. Inspect the diff, run the proving checks, compare results against requirements, and report any gaps before claiming completion.

output_examples.input: The assistant is about to say tests pass after changing code.
output_examples.output: The assistant identifies the test command, runs it fresh, reads the result, and states the exact passing evidence or failure count.

output_examples.input: A delegated agent reports that a feature is complete.
output_examples.output: The assistant checks the version control diff, verifies the changed behavior, and reports the actual completion state with remaining gaps.

output_examples.input: A bug fix appears correct after code review.
output_examples.output: The assistant runs a regression check and only claims the fix after the output confirms the original symptom is resolved.

best_practices: Run the full proving command in the current session before making completion claims.
Report command output, exit status, and remaining uncertainty in plain language.
Match each requirement to direct evidence instead of relying on general test success.
anti_patterns: Do not say work is done because the code looks correct.
Do not rely on old command output after making new changes.
Do not treat another agent report as proof without independent verification.

faq.question: What problem does this skill solve?
faq.answer: It prevents AI assistants from claiming work is complete before running checks that prove the claim.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the assistant to identify and run the correct verification commands using available tools.

faq.question: Can it be used with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when command execution is available.

faq.question: Is this only for software tests?
faq.answer: No. It also applies to builds, lint checks, requirement reviews, diffs, and delegated agent verification.

faq.question: What should the assistant report after verification?
faq.answer: It should report the command run, the relevant result, and any unverified work or remaining failure.

faq.question: Does this replace human review?
faq.answer: No. It improves evidence quality before human review but does not replace project judgment.
